equips.equip_desc:
You can change the last three items at your discretion, but at best all three should be defensive. (Since Martis is very poor in full damage.)
You might also consider purchasing a "golden meteor." Of course, it all depends on the situation on the map.

hero.hero_overview:
From the start, you should decide whether you'll get Skypiercer. If you're unsure, you can buy an axe for faster farming and higher damage in battles. (Important note: if you have Granger on your team, picking Martis isn't the best choice, as these heroes clash very strongly.) It's worth mentioning that Skypiercer is very important for this hero, as his abilities synergize very well with it.

The Trident is a very useful item, allowing Martis to kill enemies much more easily. It reduces the chance of losing his ultimate.
hero.hero_strength: The hero has incredibly high damage, and he also has the ability to not receive incoming control, which allows him to stay in battles longer.
hero.hero_weakness: Martis's main problem is his slowness before his ultimate. Any hero with proper map play will avoid this hero's clutches.

emblems.emblem_desc:
With the experience of 2000+ matches and some experiment, the perfect emblem was finally discovered.

- Fighter emblem is a must as you will be needing its spellvamp.

- Thrill for some extra damage

- Master Assassin is needed. Before going to berserk mode and 5v1, you will have to deal with a few duals (1v1), that's why master assassin is needed. To able be stand out, you need to win your lane first. 🔥🔥

- War Cry is a game-changer. It greatly boosts your damage which you will need in order to burst down the enemy.
War Cry = More dmg
More dmg = Better Execution
** War Cry = Better Execution

One example is -
1 + flicker + 2 (second phase) + Ult = Can kill full hp mm

During this combo, you can activate War Cry.
emblems.emblem_title: The Perfect Emblem for Martis Exp lane

equips.equip_desc:
1. War Axe

- Since you will be facing many team fights, buy War Axe to increase spellvamp and sustainability. War Axe is essential as it boosts your damage and gives you True Damage at max stacks.
Pro Tip: activate War Axe before going into action.

2. Sea Halberd

- Sea Halberd is also a essential item. In Exp lane, most of the time, you will be facing a lot of meta heroes with high sustain (Yu Zhong, Laupu-laupu, Uranus). To deal with this kind of situation, you will need Sea Halberd anti-heal and its passive Punish (deal more dmg to enemy with higher HP than yours).

3. Queen Wings

- For sustainability and a balance between dmg and defence. 
Pro tip: Always keep this item as third main item.

**The last two items are situational, so you can build according to enemy lineup.

🥊Pro tip: 

- Build Sea Halberd first if enemy lineup has high heal.

- Buy Sky Piercer as second item if you get fed early.

- In late game, replace boots with Endless Battle for more dmg.

data.recommend: For those who really want to master Exp lane

spell.spell_desc:
Highly recommend using Flicker. Can be used to perform amazing yet unexpected plays with Martis skills (first +flicker, second + flicker, ult + flicker)

1. First skill + flicker ( Initiation, charge)
- Use this combo to charge in towards enemy. With this play, you will be able to initiate team fights and have a better tempo. 

2. Second skill + flicker (Kill)
- One thing you need to keep in mind when performing this is that you will not immediately use Second skill, but the second phase of it. Use to quickly execute enemy.

3. Ultimate + flicker (Escape, freestyle 😁)
- This combo is rather unnecessary but in some situations, you can use this to quickly escape and cut off the distance from the enemy. Example; Tower dive.

Therefore, flicker is the best spell for Martis exp lane. If you aren't comfortable with flicker and unsure of your survival, you can choose vengeance or sprint.

hero.hero_overview:
First, to go to Martis Jungle we need the correct emblems, the best ones are: Assassin Emblem, Killing Spree and Experienced Hunter.

We'll start with the buff on the opposite side of the gold lane; take advantage of Martis' passive to farm efficiently. For our first gank, we need to ambush the enemy ADC If the enemy ADC has low health (enough to kill them with one ultimate), we'll attack them under their tower, making sure there are no other enemies nearby.
hero.hero_strength:
In the early game, take advantage of your damage and ultimate to start the kill chain. You need to know how to use your second ability, because without it, Martis is completely vulnerable.

In the late game, when your performance declines, it's important to start building a tank if your team doesn't have a solid front lane. This will benefit the ADC and the mage. Always try to protect them.
hero.hero_weakness: Martis is very vulnerable in the late game, so try to end the game as quickly as possible to avoid comebacks. Without his second ability, Martis is very vulnerable, so you should know when to use it correctly and how to time it. Heroes like Franco and Kaja counter him a lot; if you're against them in a fight, avoid being the one to initiate the team fight.

data.recommend:
Martis is very versatile; he can play in most lanes (except roam). Even in big tournaments like the MPL, Martis has been picked as a mid laner.

Martis is a good pick against heroes with a lot of CC

spell.spell_desc: Martis is a good jungler; retribution is important for him. The most efficient is blue retribution because it helps him if he gets a kill chain and can finish off the entire enemy Team.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Focus on fast jungle clear and early ganks.

Use Skill 2 to knock up and dodge crowd control.

Combo: Skill 2 > Skill 1 > Basic Attack > Repeat.

Secure the first Turtle as soon as possible.

Do not be afraid to invade. Your durability keeps you safe.

You are now strong enough to lead team fights.

Always look to start fights with Skill 2.

Use your Ultimate to secure kills and get resets.

Prioritize objectives like Turtles, towers, and jungle control.

Your tank items allow you to fight longer and still deal good damage.

Martis falls off compared to late-game carries.

Do not overextend. Wait for the right time to enter.

Target anyone low on health to activate your Ultimate.

Use Skill 2 to disrupt enemy damage dealers.

Your goal is to clean up the fight, not start it blindly.
Martis wins games by controlling mid game. Tanky items let you survive, Assassin emblem gives you kill power. Use both to dominate early, reset in mid, and play smart late.

emblems.emblem_desc:
It gives Movement Speed, Adaptive Pen, and Physical Attack, perfect for:

Invading early

Winning 1v1s

*********** fast

equips.equip_desc:
Martis is not meant to assassinate one hero and run. He is meant to start the fight, break the enemy's frontline, and clean up the rest with resets.
To do that, he needs to stay alive long enough to activate his true power: a killing storm fueled by Decimation.

So we build tank items not to play safe, but to ensure that Martis can survive the beginning of the fight and finish it strong.
It is not about who he kills first. It is about how many he kills after.

hero.hero_overview: Martis is a high-mobility fighter/jungler known for his relentless aggression and reset-heavy playstyle. His skill combo lets him dash, crowd control, and eliminate targets quickly. He thrives on snowballing with his ultimate reset (Decimation), making him deadly in 1v1s and cleanup fights.
hero.hero_strength:
Insane early game pressure

Immune to CC during Skill 2 (Mortal Coil)

Fast jungle clear

Ultimate resets on kills
hero.hero_weakness:
Item-reliant

Falls off if shut down early

Struggles vs high burst mages

And Weak in late game

dominants.dominant_desc:
You want to know some tricks for playing Martis in Mobile Legends?

Here are a few:

1. *Use your skills to harass*: Martis' skills have a relatively low cooldown, so use them to constantly harass the enemy laner and disrupt their farm.
2. *Positioning is key*: Martis is a melee hero, so positioning is crucial. Try to stay behind your minions and use your skills to poke the enemy from a safe distance.
3. *Use your ultimate wisely*: Martis' ultimate is a game-changer, but it has a long cooldown. Use it to initiate a team fight or to catch an enemy off guard.
4. *Pay attention to your surroundings*: Martis is vulnerable to ganks, so always keep an eye on your mini-map and be aware of your surroundings.
5. *Timing is everything*: Martis' skills have a relatively short range, so timing is crucial. Try to use your skills when the enemy is in range, and be prepared to retreat if they try to counter-attack.

By mastering these tricks, you can become a formidable Martis player and dominate the battlefield!

hero.hero_overview:
Martis, also known as the "Ashura King," is a powerful fighter hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang. He's known for his exceptional damage and fast fighting speed, making him a formidable opponent in battle ¹.

*Hero Overview*

- *Role:* Fighter
- *Specialty:* Reap/Charge
- *Lane:* EXP Lane
- *Win Rate:* 50.89% (Tier A) ²

*Abilities and Skills*

- *Passive - Ashura's Wrath:* Increases attack speed by 30% for 4 seconds, stacking up to 120%.
- *Skill 1 - Ashura Aura:* Deals physical damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area, slowing them by 40% for 2 seconds.
- *Skill 2 - Mortal Coil:* Strikes enemies three times, dealing physical damage and knocking them back. The third strike also deals damage and knocks enemies airborne.
- *Ultimate - Decimation:* Lunges at a designated enemy hero, dealing physical damage (or true damage if the enemy's HP is below 50%) ¹.

*Strengths and Weaknesses*

- *Strengths:* Great burst damage, crowd control, fast attack speed, and increased movement speed.
- *Weaknesses:* Low durability, weakness to crowd control, and dependence on skills ²
hero.hero_strength:
Here are Martis' strengths in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, explained in English:

# Strengths of Martis
1. *High Burst Damage*: Martis can deal massive amounts of damage in a short period, making him a formidable opponent in team fights.
2. *Excellent Crowd Control*: His skills allow him to control and disrupt enemy movements, making it difficult for them to retaliate or escape.
3. *Fast Attack Speed*: Martis has an incredibly fast attack speed, enabling him to dish out damage quickly and efficiently.
4. *High Movement Speed*: His speed allows him to rapidly move around the map, chase down enemies, and respond to threats.
5. *Strong Initiator*: Martis' skills enable him to initiate team fights effectively, catching enemies off guard and setting up his team for success.

# Synergy with Other Heroes
Martis pairs well with heroes who complement his abilities:

- *Johnson*: His crowd control skills synergize with Martis', making it easier to lock down enemies.
- *Minotaur*: His burst damage abilities pair well with Martis', creating a formidable combo.
- *Saber*: His burst damage and mobility skills complement Martis', making them a deadly duo.

Overall, Martis is a powerful fighter hero who excels at dealing damage, controlling crowds, and initiating team fights.
hero.hero_weakness:
Here are Martis' weaknesses in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, explained in English:

# Weaknesses of Martis

1. *Low Durability*: Martis has relatively low health points, making him vulnerable to damage and easily killed if not played carefully.

2. *Weakness to Crowd Control*: Despite his ability to control crowds, Martis is susceptible to crowd control effects himself, such as stuns, slows, and knockbacks.

3. *Dependence on Skills*: Martis relies heavily on his skills to deal damage and control enemies. If his skills are on cooldown or disabled, he becomes much less effective.

4. *Vulnerability to Burst Damage*: Martis' low durability makes him vulnerable to burst damage from enemy heroes, especially those with high attack power.

5. *Difficulty in Lane Phase*: Martis can struggle in the lane phase due to his low durability and dependence on skills. He requires careful gameplay and positioning to survive.

By understanding Martis' weaknesses, players can develop strategies to counter them and play more effectively with this powerful hero.

hero.hero_overview:
Martis is a fighter with good CC , AOE and a finish ult .
Passive: after using a skill he get an increase in his attack speed.
First: pulling the enemy into the middle and dealing damage.
Second: CC immunity and damage reduced while damageing
with two phases .
Ult: remove 30% of enemy's full HP and movement speed increase  , instant kill if the X mark appear if the enemy was killed by it it will be refreshed .

dominants.dominant_desc: In the early game, farm with Retribution and use Ashura Aura to poke and clear minions. After reaching level 4, roam and gank, using Decimation to execute low-health enemies. In team fights, initiate with Ashura Aura, disrupt with Mortal Coil, and finish enemies with Decimation, while relying on sustain from Thunder Belt and Guardian Helmet.
dominants.dominant_title: Before level 4, Early game and During team fight

emblems.emblem_desc: The Assassin Emblem with Thrill, Seasoned Hunter, and Killing Spree boosts Martis's damage, mobility, and sustain. Thrill increases his attack speed, Seasoned Hunter speeds up farming and jungle control, while Killing Spree resets his cooldown and boosts movement speed after a kill, making him a deadly threat in skirmishes and team fights.

equips.equip_desc: This item build gives Martis a balance of damage and durability. Tough Boots (Dire Hit) boosts defense and critical damage, while Sky Piercer and Thunder Belt enhance his damage and burst. Guardian Helmet provides regeneration, Sea Halberd reduces enemy healing, and Immortality offers a second life, making Martis a resilient, high-damage fighter.

hero.hero_overview: Martis, the Ashura King, is a Fighter known for burst damage, crowd control, and execution. He dominates the EXP Lane, clears waves, farms efficiently, and excels in team fights, using his ultimate to execute enemies and secure victories.
hero.hero_strength: Martis's strengths lie in his high burst damage, excellent crowd control, and strong dueling capabilities. His skills allow him to dominate 1v1 situations, control team fights with knockbacks and pulls, and execute low-health enemies with ease. Additionally, his passive grants increased attack speed, making him deadly in extended fights.
hero.hero_weakness: Martis's weaknesses include his reliance on close-range combat, making him vulnerable to ranged heroes and crowd control effects that can interrupt his abilities. He also has limited mobility outside of his skill set, which can leave him exposed if his abilities are on cooldown. Additionally, without proper positioning, he can be easily kited by agile or ranged enemies.

spell.spell_desc: Using Retribution on Martis enhances his farming speed and objective control. It allows him to quickly clear jungle camps, gain gold and experience faster, and secure important objectives like the Turtle or Lord. Additionally, Retribution helps sustain Martis in prolonged fights by dealing damage to nearby enemies and jungle monsters. Combined with his skills, it ensures Martis scales effectively, making him a strong presence early on and throughout the game.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Avoid team fight, go priority farm and get to Lv. 4.
Start rotating after Lv. 4,and prioritze Gold Lane. 
Get help with team with First Turtle because on early your vulnerable.

Always participate in Team Fights in this phase, because the Lord will be available after Turtle. 
Always gank and help out teammates in lane where enemy is weak. 
Help with clearing waves (not to kill but damage)

When playing burst, you should use armors in this point because your slow without passive. 
Play safe. 
Still do the following tips in Middle Game.

hero.hero_overview: Martis, a role is Fighter, and Assassin. A hero who is good on Jungle and Exp, and is great on sustaining damages, a CC (Crowd Control) on Second skill, and a finisher. Can be easily master its mechanic. Overall a good hero to use and I would recommend.
hero.hero_strength:
A crowd control skill on second skill.
Can easily wipe a team with proper use of skills. 
Sustainable, and can burst. 
Fast basic attack on Passive, and can easily farm and rotate.
hero.hero_weakness:
With no second skill, can be easily slowed down. 
Slow lol haha.
Is bad with no speed on basic attack.
Dependent on skill and passive.
